title: Support inline randomization
body:
When I'm trying to do inline randomization, some example below: 
1.  _ok = randomize(data) with {data inside {  [8'h21:8'h7E]  }; };_ 
2.  _ok = randomize(data) with { data dist {  [8'h41:8'h5a]:/80, [8'h61:8'h7a]:/20  };  };_

It generates an erro message saying: 
Calling implicit class method 'randomize' without being under class.

The systemverilog specification have a whole subsection on inline randomization. I've been part of the semiconductor industry for over 2 years now. Would love if verilator can start putting an effort to add features in regards, to layered testbenches. If there's an example out there then please do provide it. 

Verilator command: 
```
verilator --binary -j 8 --skip-identical \
		--timing -Wno-fatal -Wno-INSECURE -Wno-style -Wno-lint -quiet \
		${TBENCH} --top top \
		--autoflush --trace --coverage
```
My solver verilator: z3
My verilator verison: Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-27-g258becd17. It's a git master verison.
My OS and distrbution details: Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS

LRM: IEEE Standard for SystemVerilog—Unified Hardware Design, Specification, and Verification Language
Topic:  _18.7 In-line constraints—randomize() with_
